reviews.review_text:
Take-Two and 2K games have updated all their games Terms of Service, turning this game as well as all of their other games into literal spyware.

Important Info in Terms of Service:
• Mods are a bannable offense
• Display of Cheats/Exploits is bannable
• Forced arbitration clause and a waiver of class action and jury trial rights for all users residing in the United States and any other territory other than Australia, Switzerland, The United Kingdom, or The Territories of The European Economic Area
• You can be banned for using a VPN while connecting to online servers
• Cannot access game content on a Virtual PC

Collected Data Types:
• Identifiers / Contact Information: Name, user name, gamertag, postal and email address, phone number, unique IDs, mobile device ID, platform ID, gaming service ID, advertising ID (IDFA, Android ID) and IP address
• Protected Characteristics: Age and gender
• Commercial Information: Purchase and usage history and preferences, including gameplay information
• Billing Information: Payment information (credit / debit card information) and shipping address
• Internet / Electronic Activity: Web / app browsing and gameplay information related to the Services; information about your online interaction(s) with the Services or our advertising; and details about the games and platforms you use and other information related to installed applications
• Device and Usage Data: Device type, software and hardware details, language settings, browser type and version, operating system, and information about how users use and interact with the Services (e.g., content viewed, pages visited, clicks, scrolls)
• Profile Inferences: Inferences made from your information and web activity to help create a personalized profile so we can identify goods and services that may be of interest
• Audio / Visual Information: Account photos, images, and avatars, audio information via chat features and functionality, and gameplay recordings and video footage (such as when you participate in playtesting)
• Sensitive Information: Precise location information (if you allow the Services to collect your location), account credentials (user name and password), and contents of communications via chat features and functionality.

At this stage, I think it’s only really worth playing in co-op. The gameplay loop is still spot on, although it’s starting to show its age, the humour’s fine, but it’s certainly dated and has become a bit cringey. 

The's game is entertaining but it can get repetitive if you play by yourself. Nevertheless, it’s a good choice as a time killer, offering loads of hours of content, fun gameplay and standing as one of the cornerstones of the looter-shooter genre. A glimpse of better times before Randy Pitchford completely lost his mind.
Very humorous with gameplay that progressively becomes more difficult. Lots of easter eggs.

Obviously it's a good game. That said...

I love it when my old games get patched to add advertising for new entries into the series. Borderlands 4 is featured as a native main menu advertisement. I already own the steamy pile of shader-pre-caching that is BL4, I don't need my face rubbed in it when I try to take refuge in a better game. Randy thought the ubiquitous ads in Idiocracy was a blueprint to success.

Edit: But it is still advertising the "upcoming" Mechromancer class, which I own.

Maya is not as hot as Lilith, but this game is way better than Borderlands 1.

My only complain is that they remove Weapon Proficiency |( `O')/
Welcome to a good game absolutely bricked by a horrible patch that now adds advertisement and an eula stating that they will steal your data and bugs coming out of nowhere because of it.
This is game is soo poorly maintenanced. Still using that old 2005 program thing and makes me have to wait and reload many times to get the game to work alright. This company is horrible.

Borderlands 2 – 10/10 – The King of Looter-Shooters
Borderlands 2 is a game that defined a generation of looter-shooters and still feels like a masterpiece over a decade later. Released in 2012 by Gearbox Software, this sequel takes everything great about the original and cranks it up to absurd, hilarious, and endlessly replayable levels. With its signature cel-shaded art style, addictive gunplay, unforgettable characters, and a massive world packed with secrets, Borderlands 2 isn’t just a great game—it’s a benchmark for the genre. It perfectly blends shooter action with deep RPG progression, dark humor, and co-op mayhem in a way that few titles have matched since.
One of the standout features of Borderlands 2 is its incredible gunplay and absurd variety of weapons. The game famously boasts “over a bazillion guns,” and the hype is real. Every weapon feels distinct thanks to randomized parts, unique effects, and wild manufacturer traits—Jakobs guns feel like powerful revolvers from a Western, while Maliwan weapons spit elemental death with style. Whether you’re a commando summoning a turret, a siren phasing through enemies, a gunzerker dual-wielding everything, or an assassin picking off foes from the shadows, each of the six Vault Hunters offers a completely different playstyle. The moment-to-moment combat is fast, fluid, and ridiculously satisfying, especially when you start melting bosses with perfectly rolled legendaries.
The single-player campaign (which is even better with friends) follows a new group of Vault Hunters as they take on the tyrannical Handsome Jack and the Hyperion Corporation. The story is genuinely engaging, filled with sharp writing, memorable villains, and laugh-out-loud moments. Pandora is a living, breathing wasteland full of quirky NPCs, hidden Easter eggs, and side quests that often rival the main story in quality. The world feels massive and alive, with distinct regions ranging from frozen tundras to toxic swamps and bandit-filled badlands. The humor—equal parts crude, clever, and chaotic—elevates the entire experience and keeps you smiling even during the hundreds of hours you’ll sink into it.
Multiplayer is where Borderlands 2 ascends to legendary status. Drop-in, drop-out co-op for up to four players turns the game into an absolute riot. Split-screen, online, or LAN—however you play, the game scales beautifully. Farming bosses, sharing loot, tackling raid bosses, and screaming at each other during intense fights creates some of the best co-op memories in gaming. The endgame is incredibly deep with the Ultimate Vault Hunter Mode, countless DLCs (many of which are outstanding), and an endless grind for perfect gear that never gets old.
Visually, the cel-shaded style has aged like fine wine. It still looks vibrant, distinctive, and stylish today, avoiding the dated realism trap that plagues many older shooters. The environments are packed with detail, the animations are punchy, and the particle effects during big fights are pure eye candy. The sound design and soundtrack are equally excellent, with a killer licensed soundtrack and voice acting that brings every character to life (especially Handsome Jack—easily one of the best villains in gaming).
What makes Borderlands 2 earn a perfect score is how complete and generous it feels. It constantly rewards you with better gear, fun new builds, and hilarious writing. The DLC expansions (especially Tiny Tina’s Assault on Dragon Keep) are some of the strongest ever released. There’s very little to criticize here—occasional balance quirks or repetitive enemy types feel like minor nitpicks in an otherwise flawless package.
10/10 – Chaotic, Hilarious, and Timeless
Borderlands 2 remains the gold standard for looter-shooters. It delivers an unbeatable mix of addictive gameplay, endless loot, memorable storytelling, and pure co-op joy that still pulls players back in years later. Whether you’re playing solo or with a full squad, it’s a wild ride across Pandora that never fails to entertain. If you’ve never experienced it, you’re missing out on one of the most fun games ever made. Hands down, a must-play classic.
if you are pondering whether or not to buy the 60 dollar bundle stop pondering and buy this s teir game and all of it and the pre sequals dlcs f*cking awesome game

childhood game, it has many flaws but if you play with your friends most of the problems go away, also try to avoid being stupid while playing it, this game does need just a lil bit of a walkthrough just so you don't screw yourself over by not knowing what you're doing when it comes to leveling up and choosing proper loadout.

One of the best shoot em and loot em games out there! Super fun guns and abilities, fast paced combat depending on the area, and you can really fight the way you want. Not to mention the lovely crude humor!

I thought about writing a meme review, since there is so much material that fits for it (the game is pretty funny, and not just via its absurdist over-the-top gags) but decided that the game deserves a real review. I've mostly played as Axton, Maya, and Zero (in that order).

Pros:
- Handsome Jack (see below)
- DLCs are all solid with nice themes, and Tiny Tina's Assault on Dragon keep is excellent (see below)
- Great characterization of returning characters (Roland in particular)
- Some new characters are great/fun (Tina, Ellie)
- Character dialogue is witty and, more importantly, consistent for characters
- Art is largely improved from Borderlands 1, while keeping the core concept of the style
- Classes are all usable in some way or other, and feel unique in terms of playstyle
- Slag was a nice idea to try and add another dimension to fights
- Axton is a great expansion onto Roland's skill tree from the first game (I mained Roland)
- Maya is a great support class, and other characters have support elements in their skills
- There is no better feeling than using a sniper to cross-map a downed ally with phaselock-revive

Cons:
- Gun selection/spawns are lacking compared to Borderlands 1 (non-unique ones)
- Gunplay heavily favors certain weapon types across all classes (BL1 was class specific, at least)
- Questlines keep you in biomes for extended periods, which is sometimes fine, sometimes not
- Harder enemies make slag near-mandatory (I think it's more fun if both with and without are viable)
- Lot of backtracking in map design, not many cramped/close quarter areas like in the first game

Handsome Jack (another Pro section):
- Elite villain overall
- Great dialogue writing in both tones of the game (before and after the siren mission)
- You feel as though you can blow him off early in the game
- Gives the feeling of "I can't wait to kill this guy" later in the game
- By interaction mostly through comm, makes face-to-face meetings more impactful

Tiny Tina's Assault on Dragon Keep (yet another pro section)
- Enemies are pretty neat without trying to do too much to the game mechanics
- I GET A GUN THAT SHOOTS SWORDS?!?! AND IT EXPLODES INTO MORE SWORDS!!??!!??
- Narrative is shockingly good character writing
- In particular, [spoiler] it's a great note how only Tina harbors a grudge against Angel, since the others know Angel's background, while Tina just sees her as the reason Roland died. [/spoiler]
- My siren's name was Brick, and she was the prettiest

I have played borderlands 2, pre sequel, 3, and 4. I think bl2 is overall the best. bl3 was good and i really enjoyed it except for the main story writing. The dlc for bl3 is pretty good. bl4 has a lot of qol improvements  
and the story is alright but all of the bosses suck except for subjugator and thol. bl2 has the best raidbosses, best story, the dlc is pretty good and tiny tinas assult on dragon keep is the best of them. 
TLDR if you are only going to get one borderlands game get bl2
its a very fun game and is mst likely the best game outta all of the borderlands games i would recomened buying the handsome jack collection tho

I absolutely love this game. It is by far the best one in the series. The quests, guns, side-quests, story, graphics, GUNS GUNS GUNSSS. This is what Borderlands truly stands for and this game is the strongest release of the whole series (2026). Yes the new ones are made to be CoOL and hIP but they just turn out to be cringe. So if you're looking for a true BORDERLANDS experience get yourself this game. Did I mention GUNS? they they have like a lot of them, its kinda their thing so enjoy
The Best Installment in the borderlands franchise, I love the game in every aspect, every character is fun to play
The perfect storm of looter shooter gameplay and a fun story. Gearbox is still having a hard time trying to match both of these in newer titles.
Fun and addictive looter-shooter, tons of content, and much more. However, the scaling at OP 10 is kinda ♥♥♥♥

Gameplay design is generally dated particularly in terms of how gunplay and scaling. The story is pretty good with hit or miss humor. It's decent but I don't feel the need to come back after playing the later games in the series.

While my hours on here don't reflect how much I love this game (I played this on Xbox 360 on release date back in 2012), I cannot think of a better game that defines looting and co-op fun right out of the box. From a fantastic campaign and main villain, to a great loot system and amazing DLC, it's hard to throw this game anywhere outside of my top 10 games of all time. A true classic and worth every penny. Id go with the handsome collection as you get all of this game + the pre sequel content for a good price.

also very much dislike how elemental effect chance is tied to enemy lvl over you i currently have a run going where im almost 8-10 lvls under everything since im doing no side quests cause im a lazy bum and i cant apply any dots even with a near 90% effect chance. as once an enemy is 6 lvl over you i think its 6 you no longer can apply dots 5 lvls over and its like 80% less and so on very buns still would recommend even tho i dislike this game with every fiber of my being currently but id put another thousand or so hours in

My game time does not reflect the countless hours I've put into this game. I originally bought it on the 360 when the GOY edition released. My main is Gaige, over the years, I've been playing that same save file She's level 80 OP8 and at present on my xbox one. I was sad to learn that I couldn't transfer her to PC, but the gibbed editor at least lets me keep my customizations etc. I can't even tell you how many times my brother and I have farmed Terramorphous. This game is awesome.

Improved from the first game.  What I don't like is the level cap system where once you reach a certain level the enemies are capped and you barely gain experience and have to restart the game in different mode then you can continue leveling up.
this game is the best in  the borderlands franchise and noone can tell me otherwise i started on ps3 when i was 5 and now on pc

I've beaten this game across like 6 different platforms at this point. As you have heard, this is the strongest showing in the BL series. Iconic story, amazing DLCs, fun side quests, and some of the most fun gameplay loops with a super chunky catalog of interesting guns to satisfy any loot goblin. My only gripe is that the shooting definitely does look bleak as compared to 3 and 4. A lot of gameplay improvements were made in those games, so if you're only looking for fun gunplay, those might be better games for you.

Haven't quite beaten the game yet, but ultimately I wanted to make the review now after playing so much of it coach co-op at a friends house and here on Steam. The game is good, incredibly solid.

I've heard great things about the story, but honestly it did not blow me out of the water in any particularly fantastical way. I felt the initial opening act of the story to honestly be a bit jarring and half assed with a couple of unusual details that even after 40+ hours still don't really click, and the rest of the story suffers from relying on information provided in side quests to really bridge things together. The main quest moves pretty fast, so much so that if you aren't doing side quests you will be severely under leveled which is what happened when playing with friends. While playing solo I wanted to really invest myself into the game so I went and did EVERY sidequest that appears and the difference between the two playthroughs was quite massive. The lore for this game is AMAZING, but the story telling is meh.

Mediocre/rushed story telling aside if you like the comedy then it doesn't matter too much because ultimately the game does not take itself too seriously most of the time. They do introduce and handle dark/bleak subject matter pretty well to contrast the humor, and this can be found in every facet of the game which is what makes it work for me. The last arc of the game really does know how to make the ton shift for a moment, gets you real invested.

I do admittedly kind of dislike the loot pool though cause it can feel really arbitrary. A big thing to do in this game is hunt legendaries, but the way to do that is both kind of tedious and fairly strict. To respawn a boss you gotta quit to the main menu and log back in over and over again, but more than that the bosses do not scale with your level. Remember how I mentioned out leveling everything if you do the side quests as intended? The legendaries the boss will drop will often times be worse than an uncommon weapon of your current level, but if you do manage to get one that's similar enough to your level there is no way to hold onto that weapon you just worked so hard to get cause as mentioned the boss doesn't scale. You can't go back at a higher level to fight a higher level version of that boss to re-obtain the weapon. You have to ditch it.

That bothered me a fair bit. I did get over it a bit cause that did force me to diversify what weapons I used, and sometimes I was pleasantly surprised by what I thought I wouldn't have liked. Doesn't always work out though cause you might need to respec around the new gear instead of what you originally wanted to play.

Despite the many things I find lacking or just okay, the one thing that absolutely blew me away was the enemy design. Encountering new enemies is genuinely my favorite thing. I could honestly name every single enemy off the top of my head and you would not find that level of quality in any other game aside from Half Life 2 or Monster Hunter World. I am a simple guy, I don't care how many flaws something has if it does one thing really well. This is that one thing for me. Go play this game now.

The best Borderlands game.

Borderlands................8/10....Great vibe, gunplay and quality of life seems dated
Borderlands 2..........10/10....Great vibe, gunplay, good maneuvering, interesting characters, handsome Jack
Borderlands 3.............7/10....slightly better gunplay and maneuvering, cringy villains, annoying little girl
Borderlands 4.............6/10....slightly better gunplay and maneuvering, boring vibe, boring everything.

The Borderlands game with the best story—I sank significantly more time into it on the Xbox. For anyone who has never played this series before: please overlook the somewhat clunky controls of this game.  Endscore 10/10

In my mind, the first borderlands game was the definitive borderlands game. 2 never quite recaptured the weapon feel or the crisp balancing , or how the progression worked with weapon proficiencies. BUT, this game has always been a ridiculously good time, it was as big as it could be and still feel packed with content and secrets and of course, loot. 

This game still runs great, multiplayer works great, honestly I can still recommend this game over 3 and 4 no questions asked. 

One thing this game will always have over the first was the story, the story in borderlands 2 is extremely memorable , of all the games, this is the one I spent the most time with , and that was strictly because it was the most fun and interesting, adhd don't lie. 

You can't miss with this classic, Minion.
Extremely fun with friends. Miss playing with my trio and messing around. Always shoot the Krieg player
Great style to the graphics. Engaging gameplay.
one of the greatest looter shooters youll ever play, it still holds up even against its newer counterparts!

Came back to this game since it is one of my favorites. Mind you I've been a fan of the Borderlands franchise longer than I haven't.

A great shooter with the perfect balance of comedy and action. Love the art style and the characters + acting. Brilliant story so far. I'm not so clued in on the other games and their stories but I look forward to playing the rest. For context, I was told Borderlands 2 is essentially the 'first' game to play for the story.

Borderlands 2 is easily one of the greatest video games ever made.  I've played it ever since it released on the Xbox 360 all the way back in 2012.  Its story, writing, sound design, music, and gameplay are nearly perfect and flawless.  Handsome Jack is singlehandedly my favorite villain of all time, and yes I will go out on a limb and say he's better than GLaDOS, Darth Vader, the Joker, or Hannibal Lecter.  Jack is the twisted, egomaniacal, beating heart of Borderlands 2's story and one of the central reasons why it works.

Borderlands 2's writing does such a spectacular job at knowing when to be the most hilariously comedic game ever released and when to stop pulling its punches with extremely dour, dark, and serious plot points.  This game, from the voice lines and sounds of each playable character and every enemy you encounter to the art style to the gun manufacturers themselves and how each of their weapons behave, oozes and overflows with personality like no other FPS.  Aside from Handsome Jack, so many other characters in this game are well-written, including Tiny Tina, Brick, Mordecai, Scooter, Ellie, Sir Hammerlock, and of course Claptrap to name just a few.  And aside from their gameplay, the main cast of playable characters, Axton, Salvador, Maya, Zer0, Krieg, and Gaige, are all wonderfully written and voiced as well.  

They also have some of the best RPG-class designs in any game released.  Each of the playable characters have unique and extatically fun abilities with engrossing skill/perk trees.  I'm not sure why Far Cry 3 was dubbed "Skyrim with guns" when Borderlands 2 has been the holder of that title ever since.  Destiny and Fallout are a joke compared to how superior Borderlands 2 is as an RPG looter-shooter.  The character classes of Borderlands 2 are the mightiest in the entire franchise and are the reason why it's gameplay is so unbelievably legendary and addicting.  While I'm personally not a fan of Maya's gameplay, no other FPS experience will match that of Salvador, Zer0, or Krieg.  None.

Unfortunately, the weakest portions of Borderlands 2 are two of its central gameplay mechanics, but they aren't bad by any means and certainly not enough to prevent me from returning to this gem.  The gunplay is inconsistent.  Many firearms are fun, punchy, imposing, and satisfying to use, but towards higher levels assault rifles and SMG's are outclassed by pistols, shotguns, snipers, and rocket launchers, and their recoil and aiming stability has to be one of the worst designed in any FPS I've played.  It's not that Borderlands 2 has to be "realistic" when it comes to weapon design, but as a real-life gun owner and firearms enthusiast, it's just frustrating to see assault rifles and SMG's so poorly engage targets at mid to longer ranges because the aiming and recoil patterns of them shake in a stupid cross-cut swaying motion, rather than a predictable pull upwards.  Their damage is likewise pathetic compared to other weapon types and the other main reason why their design is so pitiful. 

The second weakest gameplay mechanic of Borderlands 2 is the level scaling past level 50.  At least for me personally, I refuse to play Ultimate Vault Hunter Mode.  Enemies are far too punishing and just about every single one becomes a bullet sponge forcing players into far too restrictive optimal playstyles.  Other than the gunplay and post-level 50 combat, everything else about the gameplay is grand slam perfect.  Enemy types and environmental level designs are extremely diverse and challenging in their own unique ways.  It's a gargantuan shame that later entries in the Borderlands franchise largely fixed my issues with the gunplay but then ruined everything else that made Borderlands 2 its peak installment.  Music is largely hit or miss.  Some tracks are mildly irritating in how bland and uninteresting they are, while others are some that I still listen to this day.  This game somehow combines Western cowboy, sci-fi space digital age, Mad Max, and military war themes in its story, gameplay, and music all in one beautiful, gory, zany concoction, and I absolutely love it.

If you've read this far into my review and still have not begun downloading Borderlands 2, stop reading and just buy the damn game already.  It is worth every single penny, and you won't regret it one bit!

Used to love the Borderlands series, but after hearing about Borderlands 4 going on some power trip, acting like the most powerful hardware consumers can buy still isn't powerful enough to run their game, and the fact that Steam continues to delete my characters in all previous versions of Borderlands has turned me completely off from Borderlands as a universe. Good job, Gearbox, you ruined the last good thing in this universe.

Had this game in my library for years and never played it till now. Its interesting how hyped the game was considering how terrible the gun play is. I mean, its a simple run and gun game, so you would assume the fundamentals would've  been handled, but no. All the guns kinda look the same and pretty much feel like styrofoam props. There's no weight or impact to them. Even the enemy animations don't react properly when hit (if they react at all), and its like your playing laser tag. Very unsatisfying. What's worse is you have to open your clunky inventory every time your 2 slotted guns run out of ammo (which is often). There you have to select other guns which have different ammo........ you know......instead of just cycling through all your weapons with the mouse wheel like every other FPS.  Talk about annoying!. 

All other aspects of the game are super polished and good. Story, visuals, sound. But I can't recommend it based on the gun play, which is crap.

I bought Borderlands 2 because I wanted to feel like a badass vault hunter, but all it has done is turn me into a disgusting little loot goblin who digs through every toilet, dumpster, locker, and steaming pile of creature waste hoping to find a gun with a prettier color than the one I already have. I have ignored actual human beings because I heard the noise of loot dropping in another room. I have killed thousands of people, robots, and animals, not for justice, but because one of them might explode into a shiny orange shotgun and give my brain the one molecule of happiness it has been missing since childhood. Handsome Jack has insulted me for so many hours that I no longer see him as the villain; he is basically the closest thing I have to a supportive father figure because at least he consistently calls me.

The worst part is that after hundreds of hours, I am still terrible. I walk into fights dressed like a legendary warlord and then get folded instantly by a rabid skag like I am a gas station napkin in a hurricane. Every time I make a new character, I tell myself I am going to try a new playstyle, then immediately crawl back to the same weapons and bad decisions like a divorced dad returning to a casino buffet. Claptrap used to annoy me until I realized he is just me: loud, needy, ignored by everyone, and one minor inconvenience away from completely falling apart. 10/10 game. Would absolutely continue ruining my sleep schedule, self-respect, and ability to experience joy without hearing a legendary loot sound.
